A record added only on our side is kept.
A record added only on their side is kept.
Identical concurrent additions collapse to one record.
A record deleted on both sides is omitted.
A delete/modify case keeps the modified record and records a conflict.
Identical concurrent edits collapse to one record.
If only their side changed, their changed record is kept.
If only our side changed, our changed record is kept.
Divergent concurrent edits keep our version in the partial result and record a conflict.
A merge with no conflicts returns a clean outcome.
A conflicted merge returns both conflicts and a partial record set.

title: The wiki must expose provenance holes as first-class gaps instead of silently omitting them, using the gap taxonomy: missing source refs, no resolving decision, no produced rules, orphan rule, orphan resolution, unreferenced source, and dangling reference.
summary: Backtraced from the Rust workspace at 80bfa00 (partition wiki); extractor confidence 0.93; upheld by adversarial refuter. Describes current behavior - human to confirm intentional or flag as accident.

evidence_references.summary: GapKind defines why a page carries a gap notice.
MissingSourceRefs covers requirements with no source refs and no references edge.
NoResolvingDecision covers resolved requirements without a resolves edge.
NoProducedRules covers resolved requirements or approved resolutions with no produced rule.
OrphanRule covers rules no produces edge points at.
OrphanResolution covers resolutions resolving no requirement.
UnreferencedSource covers sources nothing references.
DanglingReference covers references to records absent from the scope.
GapNotice is described as an honest hole rendered as a first-class notice.
Index gaps are rendered in the margin when present.
Rule page gaps are rendered in a Gaps margin section.
Source page gaps are rendered in a Gaps margin section.

title: Topic and question claims are exclusive and check-and-set: only open, unclaimed work can be claimed; claiming already-claimed work fails and reports the current claimant; releasing requires an existing claim; answering a question or closing a topic clears its claim so stale claims never survive terminal states; closed or human-blocked work cannot be claimed.
summary: Backtraced from the Rust workspace at 80bfa00 (partition cross); extractor confidence 0.9; upheld by adversarial refuter. Describes current behavior - human to confirm intentional or flag as accident.

evidence_references.summary: Topic claim normalizes and validates the actor and records a timestamp.
Closed topics cannot be claimed.
A topic already claimed by another holder cannot be claimed again.
Claiming a topic sets claimed_by.
Releasing a topic mutates the persisted topic record.
Topic release requires the topic to currently be claimed.
Closing a topic updates the persisted topic record.
Closing a topic sets status to closed.
Closing a topic clears claimed_by and claimed_at.
Question claim allows only open questions to proceed.
Questions blocked on a human cannot be claimed.
Answered questions cannot be claimed.
A question already claimed by another holder cannot be claimed again.
Releasing a question mutates the persisted question record.
Question release requires the question to currently be claimed.
Answering a question mutates the persisted question record.
Answering a question sets status to answered.
Answering a question clears claimed_by and claimed_at.
A test states topic claims are check-and-set and clear on close.
A test states question claims clear when answered.
Topic claim help states that already-claimed topics are an error showing who holds the claim.
Topic release is exposed as a command to release a claim without closing.
Topic close help states closing clears any claim.
Question claim help states that already-claimed questions are an error showing who holds the claim.
Question release is exposed as a command to release a claim without answering.
Question answer help states answering clears any claim.
Topic claim calls the store with scope, topic ID, and actor.
Topic release calls the store with scope and topic ID.
Topic close calls the store with scope and topic ID.
Question claim calls the store with scope, question ID, and actor.
Question release calls the store with scope and question ID.
Question answer calls the store with scope, question ID, answer text, and optional resolution.
A test asserts a topic claim records the actor and a second claim fails naming that actor.
A test asserts a question claim records the actor, a second claim fails, and answering removes claimed_by.
A test asserts closing a claimed topic removes claimed_by and later claiming the closed topic fails.
A test asserts updating a question to blocked_on_human removes claimed_by and later claiming it fails.

title: Rule traceability must trace from a rule to its producing resolutions and requirements (via Produces edges to the rule and Resolves edges from those resolutions) and onward to referenced sources (via References edges into the traced requirements); requirements that are resolved (by status or by an incoming Resolves edge) but have no Produces edge to any rule must be reported as coverage gaps.
summary: Backtraced from the Rust workspace at 80bfa00 (partition store); extractor confidence 0.87; narrowed by adversarial refuter: 'Computed from the canonical JSONL graph' is a read-path implementation detail; the traceability and gap semantics are the requirement.. Describes current behavior - human to confirm intentional or flag as accident.

evidence_references.summary: Rule traceability reads canonical state through StateStore.
Traceability requires the requested rule to exist.
Traceability loads graph edges from JSONL and filters them by scope.
Resolution IDs are found from Produces edges from resolutions to the rule.
Requirement IDs are found from Produces edges from requirements to the rule or Resolves edges from producing resolutions.
Source IDs are found from References edges into traced requirements.
The traced resolutions are loaded from JSONL by the derived IDs.
The traced requirements are loaded from JSONL by the derived IDs.
The traced sources are loaded from JSONL by the derived IDs.
Gap detection reads requirements and graph edges from JSONL.
A requirement is considered resolved if its status is resolved or a Resolves edge targets it.
A resolved requirement is a gap if no Produces edge leads from it to a rule.
Coverage health derives its metrics from canonical StateStore reads.
A test states rules with complete traceability are counted as complete in health metrics.

title: Every wiki page must have one canonical trailing-slash route - the scope index at '/' and each record's detail page under a kind-specific prefix keyed by the record's stable id - and the docs server and static build must agree on that convention, with the server normalizing missing leading/trailing slashes.

ScopeIndex routes to '/'.

Rule pages use the 'rules' route prefix.
Source pages use the 'sources' route prefix.
Record routes are formatted with a trailing slash around the stable record id.
